prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ARIMA', seasonality_champion='THETA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation folds show alternating trend directions (down-up-down) with significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.4648) and high seasonality variance (0.4709). ARIMA best tracks trends, THETA captures seasonality best, while FT_catboost achieves lowest overall RMSE. Early horizon specialists (THETA/ETS/ARIMA) differ from late horizon specialists (FT_rf/FT_catboost), indicating horizon-specific performance patterns. These conditions strongly support DBA combination to leverage model diversity and reduce variance.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ARIMA",
  "seasonality_champion": "THETA",
  "overall_champion": "FT_catboost",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "THETA", "late": "FT_catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["FT_catboost", "CWT_catboost", "THETA", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "ONLY_FT_rf", "ETS", "FT_rf", "CWT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_FT_catboost", "FT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "ARIMA", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "ONLY_FT_svr", "rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.4648,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "up", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation folds show alternating trend directions (down-up-down) with significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.4648) and high seasonality variance (0.4709). ARIMA best tracks trends, THETA captures seasonality best, while FT_catboost achieves lowest overall RMSE. Early horizon specialists (THETA/ETS/ARIMA) differ from late horizon specialists (FT_rf/FT_catboost), indicating horizon-specific performance patterns. These conditions strongly support DBA combination to leverage model diversity and reduce variance."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25). No leakage detected - all strategies use past windows only with n_windows=3. Score margin between top candidates is small (0.0137). Universe ranking shows baseline_mean and trimmed_mean_r0.05 as strong baselines but current set already has diversity. No pruning needed - 4 candidates of different types is acceptable. All candidates are valid from universe.candidate_names.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["trimmed_mean_r0.15", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k5"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "n_windows=3 is low, so recommended_knobs suggest stronger regularization (top_k=5, shrinkage=0.35). Current set has good diversity but universe shows baseline_mean (RMSE=20537) beats current best (RMSE=21244). Adding trimmed_mean_r0.15 provides robustness against outliers when disagreement is moderate. Adding topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 aligns with recommended_knobs.top_k=5 for few_windows scenario. n_unique_winners=8≥3 supports keeping per-horizon selection methods.", "changes": ["added trimmed_mean_r0.15 for outlier robustness", "added topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 to match recommended top_k=5"], "when_good": "When relative_spread_mean < 0.25 and n_windows ≤ 4, use stronger shrinkage and moderate top_k values."}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation folds show alternating trend directions (down-up-down) with significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.4648) and high seasonality variance (0.4709). ARIMA best tracks trends, THETA captures seasonality best, while FT_catboost achieves lowest overall RMSE. Early horizon specialists (THETA/ETS/ARIMA) differ from late horizon specialists (FT_rf/FT_catboost), indicating horizon-specific performance patterns. These conditions strongly support DBA combination to leverage model diversity and reduce variance.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=8 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity requiring diverse combination methods. RMSE spread_ratio=0.4648 and high_seasonality_variance=0.4709 support DBA for phase-shift robustness. With n_windows=3 (few windows), small top_k=3 with shrinkage=0.25 prevents overfitting. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures early horizon specialists (THETA/ETS/ARIMA) while inverse_rmse_weights balances tier1 models (FT_catboost RMSE=21817 vs worst ONLY_CWT_svr RMSE=33214). DBA_combination recommended by pattern_analyst_insights for high disagreement conditions.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25). No leakage detected - all strategies use past windows only with n_windows=3. Score margin between top candidates is small (0.0137). Universe ranking shows baseline_mean and trimmed_mean_r0.05 as strong baselines but current set already has diversity. No pruning needed - 4 candidates of different types is acceptable. All candidates are valid from universe.candidate_names.

statistician.rationale: n_windows=3 is low, so recommended_knobs suggest stronger regularization (top_k=5, shrinkage=0.35). Current set has good diversity but universe shows baseline_mean (RMSE=20537) beats current best (RMSE=21244). Adding trimmed_mean_r0.15 provides robustness against outliers when disagreement is moderate. Adding topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 aligns with recommended_knobs.top_k=5 for few_windows scenario. n_unique_winners=8≥3 supports keeping per-horizon selection methods.
changes: added topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 to match recommended top_k=5
statistician.when_good: When relative_spread_mean < 0.25 and n_windows ≤ 4, use stronger shrinkage and moderate top_k values.
